title: Links, Images, References, Autolinks, and URL Safety · Program Brief: Link Integrity
summary: Nested brackets confuse destination parsing, unresolved references disappear, javascript URLs survive into output, duplicate labels change silently, and every image receives its filename as alt text. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the link integrity increment in reference-aware accessible link and media model. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Resolve internal targets and fragments against the route manifest and distinguish build-known, external, generated, and runtime links.
Require purposeful alt text or explicit decorative treatment and preserve captions, dimensions, and asset identity separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Link Integrity · Theory Link Integrity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 5624 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve internal targets and fragments against the route manifest and distinguish build-known, external, generated, and runtime links.

A defensible solution applies Link Integrity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reference-aware accessible link and media model.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-inline-links-media-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-inline-links-media-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_inline_links_media_4(links: int = 9, resolved: int = 8, rejected: int = 1, unsafe_urls: int = 0, missing_alt_decisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if min(links, resolved, rejected) < 0:
        return False, links, "link-count"
    if resolved + rejected != links:
        return False, resolved, "link-reconciliation"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    if missing_alt_decisions > 0:
        return False, missing_alt_decisions, "media-alternative"
    assert resolved + rejected == links
    return True, resolved, "admitted-links-media"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-link-integrity-a1j2um
# Competency: ssg-link-integrity.
# Case 5305: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_link_integrity_a1j2um(links: int = 9, resolved: int = 8, rejected: int = 1, unsafe_urls: int = 0, missing_alt_decisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_a1j2um = "a1j2um-mu2j1a-5305"
    assert evidence_variant_a1j2um
    evidence_axes_a1j2um = (
        "readera1j2um",
        "source5305",
        "parsemu2j1a",
        "routea1j2um5305",
        "artifact5305a1j2um",
        "recoverymu2j1a5305",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_a1j2um)) == 6
    if min(links, resolved, rejected) < 0:
        return False, links, "link-count"
    if resolved + rejected != links:
        return False, resolved, "link-reconciliation"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    if missing_alt_decisions > 0:
        return False, missing_alt_decisions, "media-alternative"
    assert resolved + rejected == links
    return True, resolved, "admitted-links-media"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Link Integrity model · Theory Link Integrity
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Link Integrity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 6338 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve internal targets and fragments against the route manifest and distinguish build-known, external, generated, and runtime links.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Link Integrity model · Theory Link Integrity.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Link Integrity model · Theory Link Integrity, keep Link Integrity connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Resolve internal targets and fragments against the route manifest and distinguish build-known, external, generated, and runtime links.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Link Integrity model · Theory Link Integrity, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Link Integrity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Link Integrity model · Theory Link Integrity, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 6338 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve internal targets and fragments against the route manifest and distinguish build-known, external, generated, and runtime links.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Link Integrity model · Theory Link Integrity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A syntactically valid href proves the destination exists.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-link-integrity-a1j2um.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-link-integrity-a1j2um(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_link_integrity_a1j2um\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(links, resolved, rejected\) < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?resolved \+ rejected != links)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafe_urls > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?missing_alt_decisions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
